default_creds.context: compose.env POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test on cognita-db (postgres:13). Only relevant if :5432 is also exposed; not a web-UI gate. DB holds collection/data-source metadata.
INFINITY_API_KEY='test' and UNSTRUCTURED_IO_API_KEY='test' are placeholder service keys in compose.env; neither protects the Cognita UI or REST API.

install_tell: docker-compose --env-file compose.env up   (optional: --profile ollama --profile infinity; first: cp models_config.sample.yaml models_config.yaml)
misconfig_patterns: LOCAL=true shipped in compose.env enables the dangerous local-upload endpoint by default
uvicorn started with --reload in the backend Dockerfile makes any backend file write auto-execute (turns arbitrary file write into RCE)
CORS allow_origins=["*"] + allow_credentials=True (the deployed compose config) — wildcard credentialed CORS
No authentication layer at all on UI (:5001) or REST API (:8000)
Qdrant (:6333/:6334), Ollama (:11434, OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0), Infinity (:7997), unstructured-io (:9500) all bound to 0.0.0.0 with no auth in the same stack
Postgres password is the literal string 'test'

passive: http.title:"TrueFoundry – Supercharge your Machine Learning Teams"

active_probe.false_positive_check: The frontend HTML <title> is "TrueFoundry – Supercharge your Machine Learning Teams" and the favicon/theme-color (#7C20F9 purple) are shared with the TrueFoundry commercial SaaS portal — title/favicon ALONE collide with the hosted TrueFoundry product and marketing site. To pin Cognita specifically (the self-hosted OSS RAG framework), require a Cognita-unique BACKEND marker: GET /openapi.json (or /docs) on the :8000 backend MUST list the route `/v1/internal/upload-to-local-directory` AND `/v1/collections` — these vendor-unique FastAPI routes exist only in Cognita, not in the TrueFoundry SaaS control plane. Frontend-only match on :5001 with the title is a CANDIDATE; OpenAPI route confirmation on :8000 is the CONFIRMED tell. Also note the frontend build var `VITE_QA_FOUNDRY_URL`/`tf-modal-root` in page source distinguishes the self-hosted Cognita UI from the SaaS marketing page.

shodan_dorks.basic: http.title:"TrueFoundry – Supercharge your Machine Learning Teams"

deployment_tells: Frontend on :5001 with title 'TrueFoundry – Supercharge your Machine Learning Teams' and theme-color #7C20F9
Backend FastAPI on :8000 exposing /docs and /openapi.json with /v1/internal/* + /v1/collections routes
Co-located Qdrant on :6333/:6334 (qdrant/qdrant:v1.8.4) on the cognita-docker network
Optional Ollama on :11434 and Infinity reranker on :7997 when --profile ollama/infinity used
Page source contains React entrypoint /src/index.tsx, div ids tf-modal-root and promise-loading, build var VITE_QA_FOUNDRY_URL
pivot_paths: GET /openapi.json or /docs (unauth) -> enumerate full API surface and confirm Cognita vs TrueFoundry-SaaS false positive
GET /v1/collections (unauth) -> list RAG collections, their embedder configs and business context (read-only metadata)
GET /v1/data_source (unauth) -> enumerate configured data sources (S3 buckets, local paths, web URLs) revealing where the corpus comes from
GET /v1/components -> enumerate available parsers/embedders/vector-db providers wired in
Query a collection via the RAG /answer controller (read-only) to confirm a live LLM gateway and infer the upstream provider
Qdrant :6333 GET /collections and /collections/{name} (unauth) -> read vector store inventory / point counts without touching Cognita
NOTE: CVE-2025-27519 file-write -> RCE -> env-var (LLM API key) read is an ACTIVE write/exploit, OUT of read-only OSINT scope; list as known impact, do not execute
vulnerabilities: CVE-2025-27519 (GHSL-2024-194) — Path traversal / arbitrary file write at POST /v1/internal/upload-to-local-directory: user-controlled file.filename is os.path.join'd to folder_path without sanitization, enabling overwrite of /app/backend/__init__.py; with uvicorn auto-reload (default Docker config) this yields unauthenticated RCE in the backend container. Enabled when LOCAL=true (compose.env default). CVSS 9.3. Affected: all versions up to fix commit a78bd06 (PR #393); fixed only on main, repo archived 2026-03-13.

CVE-2025-27518 (GHSL-2024-193) — CORS misconfiguration: backend sets allow_origins=["*"] with allow_credentials=True, letting any attacker-controlled website issue cross-site authenticated requests to the API. Chains with CVE-2025-27519 for ZERO-CLICK RCE (victim merely visits an attacker page while a Cognita instance is reachable). Affected: same range as above; fixed in PR #393.
Public PoC available: github.com/Diabl0xE/CVE-2025-27519 (file-write -> __init__.py overwrite -> reload RCE).
auth_evidence: Default quickstart is `docker-compose --env-file compose.env up`, which exposes cognita-frontend on :5001 and cognita-backend (FastAPI/uvicorn) on :8000 with ZERO application-level authentication. PRIMARY SOURCES: (1) backend/settings.py defines no ENABLE_AUTH / JWT_SECRET / ADMIN_PASSWORD / API_KEY field — only external-service keys (TFY_API_KEY, BRAVE_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, UNSTRUCTURED_IO_API_KEY) and a CORS toggle (ALLOW_CORS default False, but the deployed compose enables a wildcard CORS config). (2) backend/server/app.py includes all routers (collection/components/data_source/rag_apps/internal + dynamic query controllers) with NO Depends(auth)/Security/middleware token check anywhere — only a health endpoint and exception handlers. (3) backend/server/routers/internal.py routes (/v1/internal/*) carry no auth dependency; the only gate on upload-to-local-directory is `if settings.LOCAL` — and compose.env ships `LOCAL=true`. (4) compose.env ships only test/infra creds (POSTGRES_USER=postgres / POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test, INFINITY_API_KEY='test', UNSTRUCTURED_IO_API_KEY='test') — none gate the web UI or REST API. A user who runs the documented quickstart and exposes :8000 or :5001 to the internet has a fully unauthenticated UI + API.
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM provider API keys: OPENAI_API_KEY, BRAVE_API_KEY, TFY_API_KEY, INFINITY_API_KEY held in backend env/process — recoverable via the RCE chain (CVE-2025-27519) since RCE runs in the backend container that holds these env vars
Ingested RAG documents / source files: anything uploaded to LOCAL_DATA_DIRECTORY or indexed from configured data sources (local disk, S3, web) — frequently internal/PII-bearing corpora
Qdrant vector store contents (port 6333, unauth by default in this stack): embedded chunks of the ingested documents, recoverable as near-plaintext
RAG collection + data-source definitions (/v1/collections, /v1/data_source): reveal what document corpora exist, their sources, embedding models, and business context
models_config.yaml model-provider routing config (which LLM/embedding gateways and providers are wired in)
Postgres 'cognita-config' metadata DB (collection metadata) if :5432 also exposed with POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
